heroHeadline: Sudan's Remote Clinics Empty as Iran War Disrupts Medicine Supply; 24+ Killed in April 17 Um Dukhun Market Drone Strikes
heroSubtitle: As Sudan enters Day 1104 of civil war, AP reports that Iran war disruptions to global Red Sea shipping are emptying pharmacy shelves at rural clinics — compounding the collapse of a healthcare system where 2,052+ have already been killed in 217+ facility attacks since 2023. Separately backfilled: SAF drones killed 24+ civilians on April 17 in Um Dukhun market (Central Darfur) and North Kordofan markets — part of a combined 44+ killed in a single day. The war's humanitarian, medical, and military crises continue with no ceasefire prospect; 34 million people require urgent aid and only 16% of the UN 2026 appeal is funded.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan, Radio Tamazuj, Amnesty International, Africanews, Wikipedia (Sudan Civil War Timeline 2026), NBC News, Middle East Eye, Deutsche Welle, Daily Pakistan, The Star (Reuters), AllAfrica, Soufan Center, AP
